objective: Prove the existing Matter particle/surface-runtime contracts as the neutral substrate, add only missing cross-lane conformance fixtures, and keep platform/application behavior outside core.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the accepted particle contract and fixture boundary.
Expose the conformance entrypoint and existing contract baseline.
Route the particle anchor/frame relation fixture without simulation ownership.
Route the visual descriptor fixture without simulation or renderer ownership.
Record the landed cross-lane particle contract routing.
Record the landed contract/fixture source map.

non_scope: Vulkan, OpenXR, Spatial SDK, Android, or renderer backend code.
Private shaders, payloads, drivers, tuning, or study behavior.
Runtime feature activation or live devices.
A new particle schema when an existing Matter contract is sufficient.

acceptance.proof: Matter particle state, config, diagnostics, render payload, and surface-runtime snapshot have deterministic valid and damaged fixture coverage.
A data-only anchor/frame relation fixture supplies situated inputs without particle simulation ownership.
A renderer-neutral visual descriptor fixture consumes Matter identity without owning simulation or backend resources.
Damaged fixtures reject app, platform, renderer, private-driver, and high-rate-control leakage.
acceptance.command: owner repo boundary and damaged-fixture gates

validation.command: focused owner crate and fixture tests
Matter, Lattice, and Optics tools/check_all.ps1 plus public-boundary and git diff checks

outputs: Cross-lane conformance fixtures and any strictly missing data-only contract fields.
Updated schema/fixture catalogs and owner routers.
Validation and rollback receipt for MOD-003 adapters.
commit_policy: Commit each owner repo after its full local gate; coordinate the cross-repo batch before adapter work.
